verses.1: “Can you draw out Leviathan with a fish hook,
verses.2: Can you put a rope into his nose,
verses.3: Will he make many petitions to you,
verses.4: Will he make a covenant with you,
verses.5: Will you play with him as with a bird?
verses.7: Can you fill his skin with barbed irons,
verses.9: Behold, the hope of him is in vain.
verses.10: None is so fierce that he dare stir him up.
verses.11: Who has first given to me, that I should repay him?
verses.12: “I will not keep silence concerning his limbs,
verses.13: Who can strip off his outer garment?
verses.14: Who can open the doors of his face?
verses.16: One is so near to another,
verses.17: They are joined to one another.
verses.19: Out of his mouth go burning torches.
verses.20: Out of his nostrils a smoke goes,
verses.22: There is strength in his neck.
verses.23: The flakes of his flesh are joined together.
verses.24: His heart is as firm as a stone,
verses.25: When he raises himself up, the mighty are afraid.
verses.26: If one attacks him with the sword, it can’t prevail;
verses.28: The arrow can’t make him flee.
verses.30: His undersides are like sharp potsherds,
verses.31: He makes the deep to boil like a pot.
verses.32: He makes a path shine after him.
verses.33: On earth there is not his equal,
verses.34: He sees everything that is high.
